name: Hojicha
foodiePairing: Perfect with toasted sourdough, dark chocolate, or roasted chestnuts. Its roasted profile complements savory brunch dishes beautifully.
description: A Japanese green tea that is roasted in a porcelain pot over charcoal, resulting in a unique toasty aroma and low caffeine content.

name: Lapsang Souchong
foodiePairing: Pairs excellently with smoked salmon, sharp cheddar, or even a spicy BBQ dish. It is the Scotch of the tea world.
description: An ancient black tea smoke-dried over pinewood fires, offering an unmistakable campfire aroma and deep, resinous sweetness.

name: Sencha
foodiePairing: Ideal with fresh sushi, lemon-glazed shortbread, or steamed seasonal greens. The 'clean snap' of its finish refreshes the palate.
description: The quintessential Japanese green tea, prized for its vibrant color, grassy sweetness, and refreshing astringency.

name: Silver Needle
foodiePairing: Best with light almond cakes, fresh white peaches, or mild goat cheese. Its subtle profile requires delicate companions.
description: A premium white tea made only from unopened buds, offering a silky texture and light, honey-like sweetness.

name: Tie Guan Yin
foodiePairing: Complements buttery croissants, fruit tarts, or lightly salted macadamias. Its floral depth stands up well to pastry.
description: A legendary Oolong tea with a captivating orchid aroma and a lingering, creamy finish that evolves through multiple infusions.
